Standard Terms of the Agreement
1. Article. Subject matterof the Agreement

1.1 By signing this Agreement. the Seller undertakes to sell theproductto the Buyer as per the terms determined in this Agreement and the Buyer undertakes to pay the product price as per the terms agreed in this Agreement.
2. Article. Product and product price
2.1 Product description/technical details: The productdescription and the details are provided in the #1 Annex.
2.2 The product price Is provided In #1 Annex. Unless otherwise specified In Annex #1, the Indicated price Includes all applicable taxes (includingvAn.

2.3 Payment conditions-theproduct price shall be paid throughthe bank transfer, on the bank account submitted by the Seller to the Buyer. In case if theproduct price is fixed in foreign currency, the Seller is a non-Georgian resident entity/person and theproduct price has to be paid in non-Georgian resident commercial bank account, the payment will be processed in agreed foreign currency. Iftheproduct price has to be paid in Georgian resident commercial bank account. the price will be paid in equivalentamountof GEL, as per theofficial exchange rate determined by the National Bank of Georgia on the day of the transfer.
2.4 Payment terms - payment terms are provided In #1 annex.

2.5 Upon the request of the Buyer, the Seller shall ensure the provision ofthe Tax Residence Certificate, issued by the relevant country, In theform satisfactory tothe Buyer, within the reasonable time from the request ofthe Buyer.
2.6 The price payable fortheproduct shall not be higher than that stated in the #1 Annex and unless otherwise stated shall be:

2.6.1 inclusive of all charges includingwithoutlimitation packaging material, packing, shipping, loading, carriage, insurance and aenvery or tne Proaucts to tne aenvery auuress ana any aut1es, imposts, 1ev1es or taxes; ana
2.6.2 fixed for the durationof the Agreement.

2.7 Seller covenants that if it should at any timepriortothedeliveryof the product sold hereunder sell similarproduct in similarquantitiesto any thirdparty at lower prices, itwillnotifythe Buyer in writingof such lower prices, and the Buyer shall be entitledto receive the fullbenefitof such lower prices from the date of such sale to any third party.

2.8 No variation in the price nor extra charges can be made (whether on account of increased material, laborortransport costs, fluctuation In rates of exchange or otherwise) withoutthepriorwritten consent of the Buyer. Any unilateral change of the price by Seller, even partial, shall entitlethe Buyer to cancel the Agreement partiallyortotally as the Buyer considers appropriate without charge or penalty.

2.9 Withoutprejudiceto any otherright or remedy, the Buyer reserves the rightto set off any amountowingor asserted to be owing at any timefromthe Seller or associated company of the Seller to the Buyer against any sums payable by the Buyer to the Seller or associated company ofthe Seller.
3. Article. Delivery terms
3.1 Delivery time-provided #1 annex.
3.2 Delivery place -provided #1 annex
3.3 Delivery expenses - all expenses related to the sale and deliveryof the product by the Seller tothe Buyer is included in the product price and shall be borne by the Seller.

3.4 Product delivery- By delivering the product as per the terms ofthis Agreement, the Seller transfers theownershiprights on theproductto the Buyer which shall be furtherconfirmed by the Delivery-Acceptance act, executed among the parties. The Seller shall also be liablefor the transfer of all relevant documentation and informationthat is reasonably necessary forthe use of the product by the Buyer.
3.5 If the purchase contract includes the provisionof services, the seller shall obey and take intoconsideration the instructions related tothe services issued by the Buyer.

3.6 The Product shall be properly packed, clearly labelled and adequately protected against damage and deterioration in transit. The packaging and shipmentof the goods (including hardware, software) and/or licenses shall be mandatorily required to conformto the laws and regulationsofthe Country ofOrigin and delivery. The Seller undertakes to pack, mark and ship the Product in accordance with the requirementof Buyer. Products delivered shall complywith the descriptionor samples or specifications provided by Buyer and shall always be subject todetailedinspection by Buyer for ascertaining whether the same are in conformitywith the requirement or not, and untilthen Buyer shall be deemed to have not accepted such Product and upon any rejectionof Product, the Seller shall be deemed to have failed todeliverthe Product In accordance with this Agreement. In such event, Seller shall at its sole discretion, be entitledtoeither purchase the Product from other sources at Buyer's cost after due noticetothe Buyer. In such an event, the Seller shall be liabletopay/reimburse the Buyer the price at which such Product has been purchased by the Buyer fromother sources. Withoutprejudice to above, the Seller undertakes toindemnify Buyer for any loss or damage suffered by Buyer in this regard.

3. 7 Insurance - Unless otherwise agreed in writing in the Agreement, the Seller shall insure the products up tothe agreed pointofdelivery and thereafteritwill be Buyer's responsibilityto insure the Products. Any insurance policy taken out by the Seller must cover all damage and loss tothe Products up thefull insurable value of the Products. Notwithstanding any otherprovisionof this Agreement, in the event of any damage to the Products before thetitle passes to Buyer, the Buer shall be entitledto receive the proceeds of any Insurance policy taken out by the Seller up totheamount due to Buyer.
3.8 Title and Risk ofloss:

3.8.1 Domestic Purchases - The titlefor the products shall pass fromthe Seller to the Buyer when the Products are delivered at tne agreea point or aenvery or wnen tne Buyer nas pa1a ror tne Proaucts m part or m run, wnicnever 1s earner. Tne nsK or loss ror the products shall remain with the Seller until the products are delivered at the agreed pointofdelivery and accepted by the Buyer.

3.8.2 International Purchases - Unless otherwise agreed in writing in the Agreement, the title and risk of loss fortheproducts shall pass from the Seller to the Buyer in accordance with lncoterms 2020 on a DDP basis.
4. Article. Representations and warranties
4.1 Each party hereby represents and warrants as follows:

4.1.1 Due Incorporation-It Is adulyIncorporated Company validly existing underthe law of Its jurisdictionofIncorporation and has the powertoown its product and carry on its business as it is being conducted.

4.1.2 Powers -It has the power and authorityto execute, deliver and perform Its obligationsunderthis Agreement and the transactions contemplated by it. No limit on its powers will be exceeded as a result of execution of this Agreement.

4.1.3 Authorisations-It has taken all necessary action and obtained all required or desirable authorisationsto enable itto execute, deliver and perform its obligationsunderthis Agreement. Any such authorisations are in full force and effect.
4.2 The Seller additionally represents and warrants as follows:
4.2.1 The sale oftheproduct by the Seller and using it by Buyer willnot result in breaching any effectiveregulationof Georgia.
4.2.2 It has provided all relevant and necessary informationabout the productto the Buyer.
4.2.3 It complies with all applicable requirements of Georgian legislation, as well as International standards and best practices governing its activities and operations.

4.2.4 The Seller represents and warrants tothe Buyer that the Products shall be of satisfactory quality, material and workmanship, merchantable, free ofdefect in design, material and workmanship and fitfor any purpose forwhichthey are intended and shall conformto the specifications and quantity set forth in the Order. In addition, the Seller represents and warrants that (a) the Products shall be adequately contained, packaged or labeled and shall conformto the affirmationsof fact stated thereon, (b) the Products willcomplywith all applicable guidelines, standards, laws and regulations, includingwithoutlimitation any voluntary codes ofconductrelating to the Products and their sale, use and supply (c) in performingitsobligations hereunder, the Seller shall fullycomplywith all applicable guidelines, standards, laws and regulations, {d) the Products or the Intended use thereof do notinfringetheintellectualpropertyrightsof any third party, and (e) any services included in the Products shall be performed in a professional manner, in accordance withapplicableindustry standards. Seller further represents and warrants that It Is able to transfer and does transfer to the Buyer good and marketable titleto the Products. All the representations and warranties set forth in thisarticle shall survive acceptance of the Products and shall be in addition to any other warranties, express orimplied, available tothe Buyer. Neitherinspectionnor acceptance ofthe Products shall impair any of the foregoing warranties.

4.3 Each party acknowledges thatthe representations and warranties under this article were Issued by parties forthe purposes of the executionof this Agreement and the counterparty relies on these representations and warranties whilesigningthis Agreement.
5. Article. Rights and obligationsof the parties
5.1 The Byer Is entitled to:
5.1.1 Request the deliveryoftheproduct as per thequality and details agreed among the parties.
5.1.2 Request the due, timely and good-faith performance of the obligations by the Seller.
5.2 The Buyer is obligated to: 5.2.1 Pay tne proauct price as per tne terms agreea in tms Agreement.
5.2.2 Duly perform all otherobligationsdetermined in this Agreement.
5.3 The Seller is entitled to:
5.3.1 Request the payment oftheproduct price as per the terms and conditionsdetermined in this Agreement.
5.4 The Seller is obligated to:
5.4.1 Deliver theproductto the Buyer at its own cost and expense and in compliance withthe agreed quality and conditions.
5.4.2 Deliver theproductto the Buyer on the agreed delivery place.

5.4.3 Ensure the dispatch oforiginaldocuments (Invoice, packing list, B/L or CMR oralrwayblll, custom declaration, Certificate of Origin, Certificate of Quality, or any other specific document required by the Buyer) through DHL I Fedex or otherequivalent international express mail service, within3working days from the date of the dispatch ofthe goods. The Seller has to send the scan copies of the same documentthrough the e-mail to the Buyer, tothe e-mail address provided by the Buyer.
6. Article. Quality and guarantee

6.1 The Seller undertakes todeliver Products of the quantity,quality and descriptionwhich correspond strictlyto the technical specifications, quantity and description In this Agreement and/orthe sample accepted by the Buyer and/or In any applicablespecificationsupplied by the Buyer to the Seller. The Buyer's weightsand/or measurements shall govern. The Products shall be withoutfault and shall be new and unused unless otherwise specified. The Seller shall complywith all applicable guidelines, standards, laws and regulations concerning the manufacture, packaging, packing and deliveryof the Products. Upon reasonable prior notice, the Buyer shall have the rightto inspect and test the Products, includingwithoutlimitation at the loading port, and the Seller shall facilitate any such request. The Buyer may return any rejected Products at the Seller's risk and expense and/or require the Seller to replace any rejected Products withina reasonable time as stipulated by the Buyer. The rightto reject shall extend to the whole or any part ofa consignment. The Seller shall reimburse the Buer for all costs, losses, damages and expenses incurred by the Buyer which are in any way attributableto the Seller's failure to provide Products which are compliant withtheapplicable technical specifications, quantity and descriptionand/or the rejectionofthe Products, includingwithout limitation any expenditure incurred by the Buyer in obtainingotherproducts to replace the rejected Products, loss ofprofits and the cost ofcollection.
6.2 Guarantee term (if applicable) shall be provided in #1 annex.

6.3 If the delivered product does not meet the agreed quality standards and criteria,within the guarantee term, the Seller shall ensure the replacement of the productwith new productof the agreed quality,within2working days from the date of the request of the Buyer.

6.4 The guarantee shall notapplyto any damages or defects oftheproduct Incurred by thefaultofthe Buyer. The fact whether the damage is caused by the faultof the Buyer or not, shall be determinedindividuallyfor each case.
6.5 The Seller shall be responsible forthecorrectionof any damages/defects at its cost.
7. Article. Qualitycontrol and product defect

7.1 In case iftheproduct does not meet the agreed criteria and quality standards, the Buyer shall be entitled to: A) Instruct the Seller to correct theproductdefect at its own cost withintheadditionaltimedetermined by the Buyer, and/ or B) Correct the product defect at its own cost and request the Seller to reimburse all such costs as a damage or deduct these costs from any payables of the Buyer to the Seller, and/or C) Ensure the valuationofthetotal costs of defect correction and request the Seller to decrease the price proportionally or deduct these costs from any payables of the Buyer to the Seller. 7.2 In case if the Seller fails to correct the product defect within the additionaltimedetermined by the Buyer, or if depending on the circumstances the Buyer has lost interest the correctionof defect (therefore determining the additionaltime is no longer relevant), the Buyer is entitledtoterminatethis Agreement unilaterally and request thereimbursementof damages.

7.3 For the avoidance of all doubts, parties hereby agree thatthefullorpartial use oftherightsgrantedto the Buyer as per the paragraph 1of this article shall be the discretionofthe Buyer and the Buyer's decision notto use these rights in full or partially, shall not mean that the Buyer has withdrawn these rightsforfuture use.

7.4 Performance defect - Parties hereby agree that considering thecomplexity and the scale of the Buyer's business operations, the valuationof the productquality by the Buyer may be time-consuming procedure. Therefore, the Buyer shall be entitledtosubmit Its claim to the Seller regarding the visibleproduct defects no later than within 30 working days from the date of executionof the final deliver-acceptance act among the parties. The Buyer shall be entitledtosubmitthe claims regarding the hidden product defects within 30 working days from the date when such defect should have been identified by all reasonable standards. Parties hereby confirmthatexecutionofthe final delivery-acceptance act and the payment oftheproduct price shall not mean thatthe Buyer has no claims in relation totheproductquality.
8. Article.Liabilityofthe parties

8.1 Parties shall reimburse each otherthe damages arising outof the non-performanceof the obligations as per the rules determined by the applicable law and this Agreement. The reimbursementof damages shall not discharge the relevant party from the dutyto perform its obligations and shall not release the breaching partyfrom any potential sanctions (includingbutnot limitedtothe penalty, etc.) determined by theapplicable law or this Agreement.

8.2 In case the Seller fails todeliver the productwithin the time agreed in this Agreement, the Buyer shall be entitledto Impose a financial penalty on the Seller as follows: For delays up to 30 days, apenaltyof 0.02% of the total value of this Agreement per day, applicable from the 1st day of delay; For delays exceeding 30 days butnot more than 90 days, apenaltyof 0.05% of the total value of this Agreement per day, applicablefrom the 1st day of delay; For delays exceeding 90 days, apenaltyof 0.10% of the total value ofthis Agreement per day, applicable from the 1st day of delay. The Buyer shall also be entitled to deduct any accrued penalty from any amount payable tothe Seller, withoutrequiringadditional consent. The Seller shall ensure payment of the accrued penaltywithin5working days from the date of such request by the Buyer.

8.3 In case if the Buyer fails to pay the product price within the time agreed in this Agreement, the Seller shall be entitledto impose the financial penalty on the Buyer in the amountof 0.01 %of the payable sum for each day of delay, until the performance of this obligation by the Buyer.

8.4 Iffor any reason the price cannot be determined,the Seller falls to deliver any one or more compliant consignments ofthe product when due, the Seller breaches any term of this Agreement, and/orthe Seller's financial worthiness becomes unsatisfactory to the Buyer, the Buyer shall have theright, In addition to all other remedies available toItunder the Agreement or at law or In equity, to (a) suspend or cancel the Agreement in wholeor in part, {b) suspend or cancel further payments to the Seller, (c) recover all payments whichit has already made tothe Seller, (d) refuse to accept further deliveries ofthe products, (e) recover fromthe Seller any expenditure Incurred by the Buyer In obtaining the products In substitution from another supplier, and/or (f) claim damages for any additional costs, losses or expenses incurred by the Buyer which are in any way attributableto the matters stated above, includingwithoutlimitation loss ofprofits and the cost ofcollection.

8.5 The Buyer's liability and Seller's recovery, for any injuries, losses, damages, expenses, costs and/orotherliabilities occasioned by, arising outofand/orrelatingto any suspension or cancellation of this Agreement by the Buyer (including any refusal by the Buyer to accept deliveryofthe Products), any breach of the Agreement by the Buyer, and/or any other act or omission ofthe Buyer (Including Its negligence), shall be limitedto the lesser of (a) the actual and direct costs Incurred by Seller for its manufacture of the Products in questionpriorto such cancellation, breach, orother act or omission, or (b) the Price of the Products actually delivered pursuant to this Agreement. In no event shall the Buyer be liabletothe Seller for any punitive, special, consequential. indirect, exemplary and/orincidental damages, includingwithoutlimitation any loss of business, profit or goodwill. 8.6 W1tnout preiua1ce ana m auu1t1on to any otner prov1s1on or tne Agreement, tne ~ener will maemnny, no1a narm1ess ana, 11 applicable and upon the Buyer's request, defend at Seller's sole cost and expense, the Buyer, its agents, servants, officers, directors and employees, the Buyer's distributors, dealers and all entitieswhich purchase the Products orproductsintowhich the Products are incorporated, and their respective customers, harmless against any suit, action, proceeding, judgment,liability, cost. damage, loss, claim and expenses (includingwithoutlimitation loss ofprofits and the cost ofcollection) occasioned by, arising out of, relatingtoand/oralleging any claim for injury, death, damage or loss to any person or any property, breach of any guidelines, standards, laws or regulations, or any consequential orincidental damages resultingtherefrom, caused or contributedto by (a) any fault, defect or alleged defect in the Products (including by reason ofstrictliability in tort), {b} any breach by the Seller ofthe Contract, (c) any breach by the Seller ofapplicable guidelines, standards, laws or regulations, (d) any act, omission, fault, or negligence of Seller or anyone acting on Its behalf, and/or (e) any Infringement,misappropriationorotherviolationof the patent, trade secret, trademark, trade name, orotherintellectualpropertyrightof any other person, firm, corporation or otherentity arising fromthe manufacture, sale or use of any ofthe Products. In connectionwiththe Products or otherwise, Ifthe Seller's employees, agents, sub-contractors or other representatives are on or present at any premises of the Buyer, the Seller shall be and is responsible for the acts and omissions of such persons within or about the Buyer's premises and agrees toindemnify and hold the Buyer harmless against llabllltyfor damage to property or Injurytoor death of persons arising outof such acts or omissions of such persons. In the event ofa claim against the Buyer which may be the subject ofindemnification,the Buyer shall provide writtennotificationthereofto the Seller. The Seller shall providethe Buyer with such reasonable assistance in the prosecutionof any defense as the Buer may request. The Seller will, in respect of any claim or suit, reimburse the Buyer forthe legal and other actual defense expenses paid by the Buyer and/orthe Buyer's insurance carriers, and for the actual amountof any settlement or final judgment award paid by the Buyer and/or the Buyer's insurance carriers. Withoutprejudiceto the generalityoftheforegoing, the Seller shall indemnifythe Buyer against all actions, claims, demands, costs, charges and expenses arising from and/or incurred by reason of any Infringementor alleged Infringementofcopyright, patent, registered design orotherpropertyright.
9. Article. Force-Majeure

9.1 The parties shall be released from performance oftheir respective obligationsunder this agreement in case and to the extentof force-majeure events, which shall include such circumstances originating beyond thewillof the respective party the controlof occurrence and operationofwhich goes beyond the reasonable abilitiesofthe relevant party, includingbutnotlimited to war, riots, civil disturbances, revolutions, strikes, lock-outs, accidents, fires, floods or other acts of god, embargoes, government actions, any legal or Illegal action or act of any third party, as a result of which performance ofobllgatlonsstipulatedunder the agreement becomes impossible. in addition, any amendment to Georgian law, whichdeteriorates legal positionof any party, affects the opportunityof performance of the obligation(s)to be fulfilled by the parties, the terms and/or expenses, includingbut not limitedto any unfavorable legal act and/oractionof any local, central, state, governmental, management, self-governmental, registeringorotherauthorities shall be deemed as force-majeure event. Any such force-majeure event shall release the parties from any llabllltyto each othertotheextentof such force-majeure event, and the term forparties' performance oftheirobligations under this agreement shall be extended fora period equal to the durationof such event.
9.2 The party affected by any force-majeure event shall notifytheotherparty in writing on the occurrence and endingof such event within no later than 2 (two) business days from its occurrence.
9.3 The rights and obligationsof the parties shall resume from the date oftheterminationof force majeure.
10. Article. Confidentialinformation

10.1 All information related to the activitiesof the Buyer that has been disclosed tothe Seller based on this Agreement, or throughoutthedurationof this Agreement, shall be considered as aconfidentialInformation. The confidentialInformation shall be the propertyofthe Buyer and any use, or transfer of such informationto any thirdparty shall be prohibited.

10.2 The Seller shall protect the confidentialinformation, shall not copy or disclose by any means and shall take all adequate measures toprotecttheconfidentialityof the informationwhich has been disclosed tothe Seller based on this Agreement.

10.3 The Seller shall not disclose or transfer the confidentialinformationto any thirdpartywithoutpriorwritten consent of the Buyer. Except for the rules provided by the law, in case if the Seller is obligatedby the law to disclose the information, the Seller shall immediatelyinform the Buyer about receiving any request for the disclosure ofconfidentialinformation and shall take all adequate measures torestrict the access toconfidentialinformationwithin the terms allowed by the law. 10.4 Upon the terminationof this Agreement for any reason, or by the request of the Buyer, the Seller shall return all copies of such confidentialinformation and ensure the removal of such informationfrom its computers/programs and any other sources.
11 Labor and Health safety

11.1 The conditions set forth in this articleapplyto the Seller only in the event that the provision/deliveryof the Goods/ Materials based on this Agreement implies/includes the performance of any typeofworktowhichthelabor safety regulatory legislation of Georgia applies.

11.2 The Seller shall complywith all applicable local leglslatlonof Georgia related tothe Health, Safety, Labor safety, Environmental Safety (hereinafterthe "HSE") includingbutnotlimited to Occupational Safety and Health Administration requirements, as well as theinstructionsof the Labor Inspection Office oftheMinistryofInternally Displaced Persons from the Occupied Territories, Labor, Health and Social Affairs of Georgia.

11.3 Safety Plan and Procedures: The Seller shall develop and implementa comprehensive safety plan that addresses the specific hazards associated with the project. The plan shall include safety procedures, risk assessments, emergency response protocols, and measures forongoing safety training and communication.

11.4 Personal Protective Equipment (PPE): All workers hired by the Seller shall be providedwithappropriate personal protective equipment, such as hard hats, safety glasses, gloves, and safety shoes and etc., whatever Is required/relevant In accordance with the legislationof Georgia related tothelabor safety, includingbutnotlimitedto Occupational Safety and Health Administration requirements, as well as the laws Instructionsofthe Labor Inspection Office oftheMinistryofInternally Displaced Persons from the Occupied Territories, Labor, Health and Social Affairs of Georgia.

11.5 Site Safety Inspections: The Seller shall conduct regular Inspections ofthework site toIdentify and address any potential safety hazards promptly. Inspections may includebut are notlimitedto scaffolding, fall protection systems, electrical equipment, and machinery.

11.6 Accident Reporting and Investigation: In the event of any work-related accidents, incidents, or near misses, the Seller shall promptlyreport them to the designated representative of the Buyer. The Seller shall also conductathoroughinvestigationto determinethe cause and implementcorrective measures to prevent future occurrences.

11. 7Subcontractor Compliance: The Seller shall ensure that all subcontractors (if any) engaged in theprojectcomplywith the HSE requirements set forth In this contract. The Seller shall maintaindocumentationofthesubcontractors' safety records, certifications, and training programs.

11.8 Safety Training: The Seller shall provide adequate safety trainingto all workers Involved In the project, Including orientationfor new hires and ongoingtraining sessions as necessary. The training shall cover hazard recognition,proper use of equipment, emergency procedures, and any specific safety protocols relevant tothe project.

11.9 Work Suspension: In the event thata serious HSE safety violationorImminentdanger Is Identified,the Buyer or Its designated representative reserves the right to suspend workuntilthe safety concern is adequately addressed and resolved.

11.1 O Buyer's rights: the Buyer shall be entitled to issue mandatoryinstructionstothe Seller ortheir representatives in respect with the HSE safety requirements and the Seller or their representatives shall strictlyfollow any such instruction. The Seller shall be liableforthe compliance with the Buyer's internal HSE safety policies and instructions, as communicated to the Seller from the Buyer's representatives.

11.11 HSE Officer: The Seller shall assign at least 1 person whowill be in charge ofthe HSE Safety duringthe provision of services. Such person designated by the Seller shall be dulycertified and accredited as per themandatory requirements ofthelegislationof Georgia, or by The National Examination Board In Occupational Safety and Health (NEBOSH) or by The Institutionof Occupational Safety and Health (IOSH). The HSE Officer of the Seller shall be directly and personally responsible for the ensuring of the HSE compliance with this contract, as well as the HSE requirementsofthe Georgian legislation.

11.12 Work accident insurance: In case ifthe works carried out by the Seller qualify as an excessively dangerous, hard, harmful 11.13 In case ifthe services provided by the Seller, includes the carrying outof certain works as a result of which theindustrial waste is produced (including,butnotlimited to: construction waste, dangerous waste, etc.) the Seller shall be under the obligation to ensure, at its own cost, the removal of such waste from the Buyer's territory and the transportationand/or disposal of such waste as per the rules and procedures determined by the legislationof Georgia.

11.14 Indemnification: The Seller shall indemnify and hold harmless the Buyer from any claims, damages, costs, expenses, loss of profit/revenue, loss ofproduction, financial loss, loss of use, loss of raw materials, loss of contracts or otherIndirect damages and liabilities arising outofthe Seller's failuretocomplywith HSE requirements or any work-related accidents or injuriesresulting from such non-compliance. Such claims shall not be subject tothellabllltylimitation clauses thatmight be agreed by parties In this contract.

11.15 Financial Penalties for HSE Violations In additionto any Indemnification or liability provisions stated herein - the Buyer shall be entitledto impose the financial penalty on the Seller for each violationof the labor and health safety obligationsstipulated in this Article 11 and/orapplicable Georgian legislation. The penalties shall be calculated as follows: For thefirstviolation-a penalty equal tonot more than 1%ofthetotal value of this Agreement shall apply; For each subsequent violation-apenalty equal tonot more than 2% ofthetotal Agreement value shall apply per case. The Buyer shall be entitledtodeduct the imposed penaltyamount from any payables that Buyer owes tothe Seller under this Agreement or any other agreement between the parties.
12 Article. Legal effect and thedurationof the Agreement
12.1 This Agreement shall take legal effect on the day ofsigning by both parties and shall be validuntilthefull performance of the obligations by parties.
12.2 This Agreement (as well as any other agreement associated with it) may be terminated based on thefollowing grounds:
12.2.1 Written agreement of the parties
12.2.2 Full performance oftheobligations by parties
12.2.3 The Buyer shall be entitledtoterminate this Agreement

prematurely, without any reason/ground, based on itsunilateral decision, by sending to the Seller the terminationnotice in written or electronic form, at least 14 calendar days before the termination date. In such case, the Buyer shall ensure the payment for the goods that were delivered by the Seller before the termination date.
12.2.4 In case due to the force majeure, parties are unable toperform the obligationsfor more than 1o days.

12.2.5 Each party shall be entitled to unilaterallyterminate this Agreement Ifthecounterparty falls to correct the performance defectwithin the additionaltimedetermined by thefirst party, or if the firstparty has lost interest in the correction and therefore the additionaltime is no longer relevant.

12.2.6 Each party shall be entitledtounilaterallyterminate this Agreement if the representations and warranties made underthis Agreement or any document exchanged by the parties turns outto be false, inaccurate or misleading.

12.3 The Buyer shall also be entitledtoterminate this Agreement unilaterallyif the Seller has passed away/is subject to liquidationorreorganization proceedings as per the law on entrepreneurs of Georgia or if it is subject to the insolvency proceedings
13 Article. Sanctions Compliance

13.1 Compliance with Sanctions Laws - Seller hereby represents and warrants that, in connectionwiththe performance ofthis Agreement, 1t snan comply w1tn an app11cao1e sanctions, exportcontrol laws, ana regu1at1ons, mc1ua1ng outnot 11m1tea to tnose administered by the United Nations, the United States Departmentof the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC), the European Union, United Kingdom and other relevant authorities(collectively, "Sanctions Laws').

13.2ProhibitedActivities- Seller shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in any transaction or perform any activityunderthis Agreement thatwould result in aviolationofapplicable Sanctions Laws by either party. This includes, but is notlimited to, dealing with persons, entities, or jurisdictions designated under Sanctions Laws as restricted orprohibited.
13.3 Representations and Warranties: Seller represents and warrants thatneither it, nor any of its affiliates, officers, directors, or employees:
13.3.1 Is a designated targetunder Sanctions Laws (a "Sanctioned Person')
13.3.2 Is owned orcontrolled by, or acting on behalf of, a Sanctioned Person; or
13.3.3 Is located, organized, or resident in acountryorterritorythat is the targetof comprehensive sanctions underapplicable Sanctions Laws.

13.4NotificationObligation- Seller agrees topromptlynotifythe Buyer partyifit becomes aware thatit or any ofitsaffiliates has become a Sanctioned Person or has otherwiseviolated any applicable Sanctions Laws in connectionwith this Agreement. 13.5 Termination Rights - In the event that Buyer reasonably determines thatthe Seller has breached this clause, it shall have the rightto suspend orterminate this Agreement Immediately upon written notice, without any llabllltyorobligationto the other party. 13.6Indemnification- Seller agrees to Indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Buyer from and against any and all losses, damages, claims, or penalties arising outof or related totheindemnifying party's breach of this clause. 13.7 Transportation of Goods - If, under the terms of this Agreement, the Seller is responsible fortransporting the purchased Goods to the Buyer's address or any mutually agreed destination,the Seller shall also adhere to the followingobligations: 13.7.1Prohibited Routes - The Seller shall nottransport the goods throughtheterritoriesof the Russian Federation, the Islamic Republic of Iran, North Korea, or any othercountrycurrentlyunderinternational sanctions that may affect the deliveryof goods. 13.7.2 Route Confirmation- The Seller is obligatedtoprovide the Buyer withadetailedtransportationroutepriorto commencing the shipment and must obtainwrittenor e-mail confirmationof approval from the Buyer.
